_meta.description: Ground-facing degree day calculations. These approximate heat transfer through ground-contact surfaces using a constant ground temperature of 10 deg-C. HDD uses heating setpoint and heating season days. CDD behavior varies by thermal capacitance mode.

inputs.name: climate.heating.setpoint
inputs.description: Heating setpoint (h_23) -- used by GF HDD

inputs.name: climate.cooling.setpoint
inputs.description: Cooling setpoint (h_24) -- used by GF CDD

inputs.name: climate.coolingDays
inputs.description: Cooling season days (m_19) -- used by both

inputs.name: building.capacitance.setting
inputs.description: Static or Capacitance (h_21) -- used by GF CDD only

inputs.name: building.capacitance.percentage
inputs.description: Capacitance percentage (i_21) -- dependency declared but not used in current compute

invariants.description: Heating and cooling seasons partition the year

behavior.note: heatingDays = 365 - coolingDays. Result is rounded to integer.
Static mode floors at zero -- ground cannot cause cooling load when ground is warmer than setpoint
Capacitance mode allows negative values -- ground absorbs excess heat

edgeCases.rationale: Ground at 10 deg-C is cooler than setpoint, so ground provides free cooling in capacitance mode
No cooling season means all days are heating days
No temperature differential means no heat loss to ground
